summary: Fix validity window in beforeCreate/beforeUpdate, then derive record timestamps from tcx
detail: When an on-chain policy requires a timestamp field (createdAt, updatedAt) to exactly equal the transaction's validity boundary, use the beforeCreate/beforeUpdate hook to: (1) call tcx.validFor(duration) to fix the validity window, (2) read tcx.txnEndTime (or compute from the start), (3) return a new record with the timestamp set. This ensures the off-chain record field and on-chain validity range are coordinated in a single choke point. Use txnEndTime for pessimistic 'certainly not after' semantics; use the validity start for 'certainly not before' semantics.
